title: PROP-026 M1 routing-matrix extension: add route-to-analyst + route-to-curmudgeon actions, with PROP-025 class-field propagation through the routing chain
authored_by: tinker (2026-05-10 run, in response to DIRECTIVE-20260510-002)
amends: PROP-026 (matrix gap; this PROP adds two new actions to PROP-026's M1 severity-tier matrix and codifies the PROP-025 class-field propagation)
target: monitor/prompts/decider.md (Priority 5b matrix extension + new bash helper conditional + LLM override path docs) + monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md (Step 8c carry-over enforcement gets class_hint logic) + monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md (intake reads class_hint) + monitor/prompts/reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md (closure-ledger schema extension; ISS class_hint field) + new monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md (single-page reference for the routing chain)

executive_summary.problem_one_line: PROP-026 M1 matrix has only three actions (PATCH | WONTFIX | ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN); first burndown fire (2026-05-10T07:58Z) over-escalated 9 of 9 moderates because the matrix had no structured route-to-analyst path. Operator manually re-routed all 9 to assigned-analyst.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Add ROUTE-TO-ANALYST and ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON as first-class actions in M1's matrix; flip the moderate default from ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN to ROUTE-TO-ANALYST; allow auto-PATCH on moderates that pass a three-rule narrowness gate (single-line + re-grep present + NOT in NEVER_PUSH); carry an advisory class_hint along the M1 → ISS → analyst EXP → queue push → curmudgeon chain so analyst-driven outputs don't default to deep-attack singletons.

tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q1_matrix_scope: Implement BOTH route-to-analyst AND route-to-curmudgeon in a single coherent commit. Route-to-curmudgeon will be rarely-used (today's 9-ISS batch produced zero curmudgeon-routes), but skipping it would leave a hole that has to be filled later under the same class-propagation design — bundling avoids two prompt-churn cycles.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q2_moderate_narrow_patch_test: Three-rule conjunctive gate: (a) NARROWNESS — ISS body explicitly identifies a single-field, single-line, or 2-3-character correction; (b) RE-GREP — the offending text/condition is still present in the live target; (c) NOT_NEVER_PUSH — target path is not in workspace-sync NEVER_PUSH. All three must hold. If any fails: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST (not escalate-to-human; ROUTE-TO-ANALYST is the new default).
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q3_default_action_flip: YES — flip moderate default from ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN to ROUTE-TO-ANALYST. Evidence: 9/9 = 100% over-escalation rate on first fire. Operator's manual re-route confirmed 8/9 were analyst-class work. The 1/9 that correctly stayed pending-human (ISS-1089) hit NEVER_PUSH and would route correctly under the new matrix anyway.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q4_class_hint_placement: On the ISS itself as a new optional field `class_hint: 'verification' | 'deep-attack' | 'holistic' | null`, mirrored on the closure-ledger entry under `closure_evidence.class_hint` for audit. Analyst reads ISS at intake; closure-ledger preserves the routing decision's hint as historical record.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q5_reference_doc_scope: Single new file `monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md` capturing the 5-action decision tree + class-hint propagation chain. Linked from decider.md Priority 5b, decider-curmudgeon.md Step 8c, and analyst-mode1-expansions.md intake section. Replaces inline duplication.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q6_closure_ledger_schema: YES — extend `action_taken` enum to include 'route-to-analyst' and 'route-to-curmudgeon' (alongside existing 'patch', 'wontfix', 'escalate'). Add 'narrow-patch' as a distinct action_taken value when the moderate-narrow path fires (vs vanilla 'patch' on minor). Distinct values let the revert script distinguish closure types.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q7_curmudgeon_side_tagging_for_routed_wo: PROP-025's existing analyst-declares-review_class discipline is sufficient as the load-bearing tag. The class_hint added by PROP-027 is an advisory upstream signal; analyst overrides as authoritative when authoring the EXP. Decider's existing pass-through (read exp.review_class → push queue with class=exp.review_class) does not change. No additional helper signals required.

outcomes.over_escalation_rate: 100% — all 9 escalations were analyst-class work, not operator-only

the_revised_matrix.summary: PROP-026's three-action M1 matrix becomes a five-action decision tree. The decision proceeds top-down per ISS — first match wins. Defaults are documented but the LLM may override per-issue with rationale.

action_set.PATCH: Decider directly applies a find/replace fix this run. Closes ISS with fixed_by='M1-patch'.
action_set.NARROW-PATCH: Decider directly applies a single-line/single-field fix on a moderate-severity ISS that passed the three-rule narrowness gate. Closes ISS with fixed_by='M1-narrow-patch' (distinct from PATCH so audit can track the moderate-via-narrow path separately).
action_set.WONTFIX-WITH-RATIONALE: Decider re-greps the live target, confirms the issue is no-longer-real (text rewritten, EXP integrated, condition met), closes ISS with fixed_by='M1-wontfix' and explicit rationale. FORBIDDEN on moderate severity.
action_set.ROUTE-TO-ANALYST: Decider sets ISS status='assigned-analyst', adds routing_reason field explaining the analyst-class work expected, and (optional) class_hint field. Analyst picks up via Mode 2 (assigned-analyst) intake on next run.
action_set.ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON: Decider pushes a queue item to monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json with target_type appropriate to the ISS (verdict-review | win-detail-rewrite | section-rewrite | etc.) and class set per PROP-025 rules ('verification' for re-verifying patches; 'deep-attack' for re-attack on substantive concern; 'holistic' for cross-cutting). Closes the M1 candidate-loop entry; the queue push is the new state. ISS stays 'open' but flagged with `routed_to_curmudgeon_queue_id: <queue_id>` so it doesn't re-trigger M1 next run.
action_set.ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN: Renamed from current 'escalate' to be explicit. Used ONLY when operator-only judgment is required (legal/strategic/personal knowledge, physical-world verification, NEVER_PUSH file modification, major/critical verdict commit). The default action is no longer ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN — it is ROUTE-TO-ANALYST.

decision_tree_top_down.rationale: PROP-026 invariant — never auto-close major/critical. Unchanged.

decision_tree_top_down.rule: Re-grep confirms the issue is no-longer-real (text rewritten, EXP integrated, condition met) AND severity ∈ {minor, info}
decision_tree_top_down.rationale: PROP-026 invariant — wontfix forbidden on moderate. Unchanged. Re-grep evidence required in the closure ledger.

decision_tree_top_down.rule: Issue passes the three-rule narrowness gate (NARROWNESS + RE-GREP + NOT_NEVER_PUSH) — see narrowness_gate section
decision_tree_top_down.rationale: Mechanical fixes don't need analyst routing. Narrow-patch on moderate is the new path Q2 asks for; it required the three-rule gate to be safe.

decision_tree_top_down.rule: Issue requires NEVER_PUSH file modification, physical-world verification, legal/strategic/personal knowledge, or operator-applied infra patch
decision_tree_top_down.rationale: Genuine operator-only territory. ISS-1089 (build-scripts/generate-html.js patches), ISS-1924 (physical figure verification) are the canonical examples.

decision_tree_top_down.rule: Issue is a curmudgeon-raised carry-over re-attackable by curmudgeon (post-patch verification, holistic check that fell through queue, c-N finding curmudgeon wants to re-attack on c-(N+1) but never got bumped)
decision_tree_top_down.rationale: Curmudgeon is the right consumer when the next action is 'attack the target with the existing argument' rather than 'author new content'. Queue push class derived per PROP-025: 'verification' if confirming patch landed; 'deep-attack' if re-flagging substantive concern.

decision_tree_top_down.rule: Default — issue requires design judgment, source investigation, framing decision, EXP revision, multi-issue cluster re-evaluation, or analyst-class defense of a curmudgeon-raised concern
decision_tree_top_down.rationale: This is the NEW default for moderates and the unrefined-minor case. Replaces the previous 'escalate-to-human' default. The routing_reason field captures the per-issue rationale; class_hint provides the optional advisory tag for downstream PROP-025 propagation.

narrowness_gate.intent: Allow auto-PATCH on moderate when the LLM has explicit narrowness evidence. Without this gate, all moderates default to ROUTE-TO-ANALYST (better than today's ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN default but still slower than necessary for clearly-mechanical moderates).

three_rules_conjunctive.definition: The ISS body explicitly identifies a single-field correction, a single-line text replacement, a 2-3-character fix, or a one-token rename. The LLM has line-level evidence in the description (or via re-read of the source). Multi-paragraph rewrites, semantic-judgment changes, or any change requiring 'pick the right phrasing' do NOT pass.
pass_examples: ISS-1043: WIN-044 code_analysis.monitoring tagged 'none' but should be 'hardcoded' — single field, value pinned by ISS body.
Hypothetical: 'docs/index.html line 1247 says "68 WINs" but should say "71" per current count.' — single literal, value pinned.
fail_examples: Hypothetical: 'Section 4 framing should better acknowledge the methodology caveat.' — requires phrasing judgment.
Hypothetical: 'Add a paragraph explaining the OTS error.' — multi-sentence prose addition.

three_rules_conjunctive.definition: Run grep against the live target file. The exact text or condition the ISS describes must still be present. If the text was already rewritten or removed: this is WONTFIX territory (step 2), not narrow-patch.
three_rules_conjunctive.implementation_hint: The decider LLM walking the M1 candidate list runs `grep -n 'exact_text' <target_file>` on the path the ISS references. If grep returns zero hits, route to WONTFIX-WITH-RATIONALE (or ROUTE-TO-ANALYST if the issue body is ambiguous about what 'no-longer-real' would look like).

three_rules_conjunctive.definition: The target file path is NOT in the workspace-sync NEVER_PUSH deny-list (currently: docs/*, build-scripts/*, build.js, CLAUDE.md, monitor/v6-restructure-map.json, test.js, monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl, monitor/curmudgeon/pending-digest.json, monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag, decider-mode.json, closure-ledger.jsonl, monitor/prompts/*.md). NEVER_PUSH files cannot round-trip from FUSE → main; decider can still patch in its working clone, but the patch goes through PROP-016 stranded-patches workflow (operator applies in own-clone-with-direct-push). For M1 narrow-patch, that stranded-patches detour breaks the auto-close model — better to ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN explicitly so the operator handles the apply.
three_rules_conjunctive.the_canonical_NEVER_PUSH_list: Source of truth is monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH=(...) array. Decider should read it at run start (or inline-encode and re-verify against workspace-sync.md as a self-test).
three_rules_conjunctive.exception: data/* files in NEVER_PUSH-style paths but actually data — no, there are none currently. data/wins.json, data/sections.json, etc. are universal-pusher-eligible per CLAUDE.md File Ownership.

narrowness_gate.if_all_three_pass: Action = NARROW-PATCH on moderate, PATCH on minor/info. Closure-ledger entry uses action_taken='narrow-patch' (distinct value for audit).
narrowness_gate.if_any_fails: Default to ROUTE-TO-ANALYST (NOT escalate-to-human). The NEVER_PUSH failure case may also legitimately route to ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN if the operator-applied infra patch is the only path forward (per step 4 of decision tree). LLM judgment per-issue.

routing_decision_judgment_analyst_vs_cur.directive_question: What signals trigger route-to-analyst vs route-to-curmudgeon?
routing_decision_judgment_analyst_vs_cur.tinker_recommendation: LLM in-context judgment per ISS, with the following defaults to anchor the decision tree.

default_routing_by_source.reason: Analyst owns design/source/framing work; these sources naturally produce analyst-class items.

default_routing_by_source.source: curmudgeon-review (carry-over still open from prior cycle, same finding)
default_routing_by_source.reason: Re-attack same target, decider has likely patched; verify the patch landed.

default_routing_by_source.source: curmudgeon-review (substantive concern requiring DEFENSE or REVISION of the prose/argument)
default_routing_by_source.reason: Analyst authors the defense or the rewrite; re-routing to curmudgeon would just produce the same hole on next cycle. The c4→c5→c6 ratchet pattern PROP-026 M3 was designed to prevent.

default_routing_by_source.source: curmudgeon-review (post-patch verification ONLY — patch already landed, ISS just unclosed)

routing_decision_judgment_analyst_vs_cur.rejected_alternative: Hard rule 'source X always routes to Y' — too rigid. Many curmudgeon-raised ISSs blur the line (ISS-1164 is a worked example: curmudgeon raised it but the next action is analyst-class defense or revision, not curmudgeon re-attack).
routing_decision_judgment_analyst_vs_cur.operator_framing_resolution: Per the directive Q1: 'Most of these check values aren't things i do, it's easy things for one of the agent.' The matrix recognizes that analyst-class work is the dominant case and makes it the default; curmudgeon-class is the exception path.
routing_decision_judgment_analyst_vs_cur.moderate_narrowness_dominates: When the narrowness gate passes, the analyst/curmudgeon decision is moot — patch directly. The routing decision only fires when narrowness gate fails AND severity allows it (not major/critical) AND not NEVER_PUSH territory.

class_hint_propagation_chain.summary: An optional advisory tag flows from M1 decision-time → ISS field → analyst EXP authoring → queue push → curmudgeon Step 8a batch gate. The tag is non-binding at the analyst layer (PROP-025 makes review_class authoritative there); it is informational, intended to save analyst per-issue cognitive load when the routing context already implies a class.

where_the_hint_lives.primary_location: On the ISS itself as a new optional field `class_hint: 'verification' | 'deep-attack' | 'holistic' | null`. Decider sets at M1 routing time.
where_the_hint_lives.audit_mirror: On the closure-ledger entry (the M1 routing record) under `closure_evidence.class_hint`. Preserves the routing decision's hint as a historical record, useful when reverting or auditing the M1 decision.

rejected_locations.in_routing_reason_freetext: Brittle. The string format would have to be parsed; analyst would have to grep for it; PROP-025's structured class field is the precedent that says 'use a structured field, not freetext.'
rejected_locations.on_the_closure_ledger_entry_only: Analyst doesn't read the closure ledger at intake; the hint must be co-located with the ISS the analyst reads.

the_chain_step_by_step.action: When taking ROUTE-TO-ANALYST: write `class_hint` field on the ISS along with `status='assigned-analyst'`, `routing_reason`, `routed_at`, `routed_by_run`. Hint values: 'verification' if narrow-correction work / value-fact-check / single-source-investigation; 'deep-attack' if EXP revision / new argument / defender-pivot / curmudgeon-raised-concern-needing-defense; 'holistic' if multi-WIN or cross-section work; null if uncertain.
the_chain_step_by_step.where: monitor/prompts/decider.md Priority 5b LLM override path; monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md (canonical reference)
the_chain_step_by_step.binding: advisory; analyst overrides at EXP authoring

the_chain_step_by_step.step: Analyst reads the hint at intake
the_chain_step_by_step.action: Read `iss.class_hint` along with `iss.routing_reason`. If present, factor into review_class decision when authoring the EXP. If absent, decide review_class from EXP scope per PROP-025 author guidance.
the_chain_step_by_step.binding: advisory; analyst's review_class is authoritative on the EXP file

the_chain_step_by_step.action: Set `review_class` on EXP-NNN.json per PROP-025 author guidance. The hint is one input; scope of work is the dominant input. If hint='verification' but EXP introduces new arguments, override to 'deep-attack'; if hint='deep-attack' but EXP turns out to be pure refinement, override to 'verification'.
the_chain_step_by_step.binding: PROP-025 invariant — analyst's call is final on the EXP file

the_chain_step_by_step.step: Decider integrates the EXP and pushes the queue
the_chain_step_by_step.action: Read `exp.review_class`; push queue item with `class=exp.review_class`. PROP-025 unchanged. The class_hint round-trips informationally (decider may log it in the daily report under m1_followups for audit) but does NOT change the queue push class.
the_chain_step_by_step.where: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md Step 7 EXP integration push (PROP-025 already covers this)
the_chain_step_by_step.binding: decider does pure pass-through; class_hint is no longer load-bearing once exp.review_class is set

the_chain_step_by_step.step: Curmudgeon reads queue item.class at Step 8a
the_chain_step_by_step.action: PROP-025 unchanged — read `item.class`, batch verifications (≤20 KB combined), singleton deep-attack and holistic.

M3_carryover_route_to_curmudgeon_class_d.summary: When M3 (PROP-026 carry-over enforcement) takes the ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON terminal action (a path PROP-026 enabled in concept but PROP-027 makes structured), the queue push class is derived from the carry-over's nature.

rules.carry_over_nature: Pure 'verify the c-N issue is closed' — patch landed in c-(N-1), c-N is mechanical confirmation
rules.rationale: Batchable; deep work happened in c-(N-1).

rules.carry_over_nature: Re-flag a substantive concern that decider hasn't acted on, AND the next action is curmudgeon re-attack (vs analyst defense)
rules.rationale: New argument or sharpened attack expected.

rules.carry_over_nature: Cross-WIN or cross-section concern (rare for carry-overs but possible)

M3_carryover_route_to_curmudgeon_class_d.fallback_default: verification (the most common carry-over case is post-patch confirmation; biasing toward batchable saves Opus cycles).

M1_route_to_curmudgeon_class_derivation.summary: When M1 directly takes ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON (rare path; today's 9-ISS batch had zero), the same rules apply.
M1_route_to_curmudgeon_class_derivation.default: verification when the routed work is post-patch confirmation; deep-attack if substantive re-attack.

worked_examples_today_s_9_ISSs.intro: Each of the 9 ISSs operator manually re-routed on 2026-05-10T08:25Z is evaluated under the revised matrix. The decision tree's first matching action is the routing.

matrix_walk: Step 1 (major/critical) — fail (moderate)
Step 2 (re-grep negative AND minor/info) — N/A on moderate

Step 3 (narrowness gate): NARROWNESS pass (single-field 'none' → 'hardcoded'), RE-GREP — pending re-grep against monitor.py + wins.json, NOT_NEVER_PUSH — wins.json eligible (universal-pusher) → all three rules likely pass
Action: NARROW-PATCH (decider direct, single-field code_analysis.monitoring update)
ISS_1043_WIN044_monitoring_tag.delta_from_current: Currently routed to analyst (manual operator re-route). Under new matrix: decider patches directly without analyst hop. Net throughput gain: 1 fewer agent cycle.

matrix_walk: Step 3 narrowness gate: NARROWNESS — adds a clause, multi-word phrasing decision (not single-field); FAIL

Step 4 (NEVER_PUSH / physical-world / legal): docs/index.html — IS in NEVER_PUSH BUT this is a content file decider can patch in working clone and push (PROP-016 stranded-patches if direct push fails). The NEVER_PUSH check here is more about 'can decider auto-close?' — for content patches, yes. So step 4 fails unless we tighten.
Step 6 default: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST
ISS_1087_TS_tab_methodology_caveat.design_clarification_needed: docs/index.html is in NEVER_PUSH but is also the canonical content file. The narrowness rule's NOT_NEVER_PUSH condition needs sub-rule: 'docs/index.html for prose content goes through stranded-patches; M1 narrow-patch is allowed if operator has accepted the stranded-patches workflow.' Currently operator does accept it (PROP-016 Mech A). Recommendation: TREAT docs/index.html as patch-eligible for M1 narrow-patch ONLY when the patch is a single-line text replacement. If the patch is a clause-addition (this case), it requires phrasing judgment → ROUTE-TO-ANALYST regardless.
ISS_1087_TS_tab_methodology_caveat.class_hint_at_routing: verification (analyst confirms exact wording, decider patches)
ISS_1087_TS_tab_methodology_caveat.delta_from_current: Same outcome (analyst routing). Class_hint='verification' new — saves analyst cognitive load deciding review_class on the eventual EXP.

matrix_walk: Step 3 narrowness gate: NARROWNESS — replaces 'rhetorical' with a different framing, multi-word phrasing decision; FAIL
Step 6 default: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST
Step 3 narrowness gate: NOT_NEVER_PUSH — build-scripts/generate-html.js IS in NEVER_PUSH; FAIL
Step 4 (NEVER_PUSH operator-applied): TRUE — generate-html.js requires operator apply
Action: ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN
ISS_1089_EXP175_generate_html_patches.delta_from_current: No change. New matrix correctly preserves the escalation.

matrix_walk: Step 3 narrowness gate: NARROWNESS — adds 6 author_* fields, multi-field schema design; FAIL
Step 5 (curmudgeon re-attack source) — N/A (decider-self-tagged source on EXP integration)
Step 6 default: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST
ISS_1130_EXP183_schema_extension.class_hint_at_routing: deep-attack (introduces new schema fields with downstream display implications)
ISS_1130_EXP183_schema_extension.delta_from_current: Same routing. Hint added; saves analyst cognitive load.

matrix_walk: Step 3 narrowness gate: NARROWNESS — requires re-greping three sources (ai_manifest, visible grid, claim_index) to find canonical citation; multi-source investigation; FAIL
Step 6 default: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST
ISS_1132_three_layer_count_triangulation.delta_from_current: Same routing. Hint='verification' — once analyst returns the canonical citation, the fix is mechanical decider patch.

matrix_walk: Step 3 narrowness gate: NARROWNESS — schema fields added to E_PRIMARY; FAIL (multi-field)
Step 6 default: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST
ISS_1147_EXP183_E_PRIMARY_deferred.class_hint_at_routing: deep-attack (coupled with ISS-1130 cluster, schema-extension work)

matrix_walk: Step 5 (curmudgeon re-attack vs analyst defense): the next action is analyst defense/revision of the registry-framing claim — NOT curmudgeon re-attack. The curmudgeon raised the concern; the curmudgeon doesn't have new evidence to attack with. → ROUTE-TO-ANALYST, not ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON
Step 6 default: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST
ISS_1164_Part46_Registry_Framing_Audit.class_hint_at_routing: deep-attack (substantive concern requires new defense argument or rewrite)
ISS_1164_Part46_Registry_Framing_Audit.delta_from_current: Same routing. The curmudgeon-raised source could superficially have suggested ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON, but step 5's 'next action' criterion correctly classifies as analyst-class. Hint='deep-attack' alerts analyst that the eventual EXP is not just refinement.
ISS_1164_Part46_Registry_Framing_Audit.edge_case_note: This is the 'blurs the line' case the directive flagged. The decision rule that resolves it: focus on what the next action IS (defense / revision authoring → analyst), not who raised the concern (curmudgeon → not necessarily curmudgeon).

matrix_walk: Step 3 narrowness gate: NARROWNESS — clarify which count is authoritative (6 vs 8-9), then decider patches; PARTIAL — single-source clarification but the analyst must triangulate first
Step 6 default: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST
ISS_1165_Part46_count_consistency.delta_from_current: Same routing. Hint='verification' — analyst returns canonical count, decider patches.

matrix_walk: Step 5 (curmudgeon re-attack): next action is analyst defense or revision — N/A
Step 6 default: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST
ISS_1166_Part46_load_bearing_headline.class_hint_at_routing: deep-attack (defends or revises a load-bearing assertion)

summary_under_revised_matrix.delta_from_first_burndown_fire: First fire (no PROP-027): 9/9 escalate-to-human, 0/9 routed correctly. Under PROP-027: 8/9 ROUTE-TO-ANALYST (correct), 1/9 NARROW-PATCH (saves an analyst hop), 1/9 ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN (correct retention — NEVER_PUSH file). Net: matrix correctly classifies all 10 (9 + ISS-1089 already correctly classified).

closure_ledger_schema_extension.current_per_line_schema_PROP_026: { closed_at, closed_by_run, closed_by_mechanism: 'M1'|'M2'|'M3', iss_id, prior_status, closure_reason, closure_evidence: { ... }, can_revert, dryrun }

action_taken_field_added_to_top_level.rationale: Currently the closure_evidence.action_intent and the bash helper's `action` variable carry this; promoting to top-level is cleaner for revert script + monthly tinker audit. Distinct 'narrow-patch' value lets audit track the moderate-narrow path separately from minor patches (key risk: narrow-patch on moderate is the riskiest auto-action; want to know its frequency).

route_to_analyst_specific_fields.fields: Under top-level: route_target_status: 'assigned-analyst' (always for this action), class_hint: <enum>|null. Under closure_evidence: class_hint mirror (audit redundancy)
route_to_analyst_specific_fields.example: { "closed_at": "...", "closed_by_mechanism": "M1", "action_taken": "route-to-analyst", "iss_id": "ISS-1130", "route_target_status": "assigned-analyst", "class_hint": "deep-attack", "closure_reason": "M1: design judgment / EXP-183 revision", "closure_evidence": { "age_days": 21, "severity": "moderate", "class_hint": "deep-attack", "matrix_step": 6, "description_excerpt": "..." }, "can_revert": true, "dryrun": false }

route_to_curmudgeon_specific_fields.fields: Under top-level: route_queue_id: <int> (queue_id of pushed item), route_queue_class: 'verification'|'deep-attack'|'holistic'. Under closure_evidence: same mirror.
route_to_curmudgeon_specific_fields.example: { "closed_at": "...", "closed_by_mechanism": "M1", "action_taken": "route-to-curmudgeon", "iss_id": "ISS-NNNN", "route_queue_id": 318, "route_queue_class": "verification", "closure_reason": "M1: post-patch verification — push for c-N+1 confirmation", "closure_evidence": { "age_days": 24, "severity": "minor", "queue_class": "verification", "queue_target_type": "win-detail-rewrite", "matrix_step": 5 }, "can_revert": true, "dryrun": false }

narrow_patch_specific_fields.fields: Under closure_evidence: narrowness_gate: { narrowness: bool, regrep: bool, not_never_push: bool, all_pass: bool }. Audit can verify the gate fired correctly.
narrow_patch_specific_fields.rationale: Risk surface — narrow-patch on moderate is the new automatic write path. Recording the gate result on every closure ledger entry lets a monthly tinker audit verify the gate pass-rate matches expectations.

revert_script_compatibility.existing: build-scripts/revert-burndown-closures.js currently filters by run_id and re-opens listed ISSs.
revert_script_compatibility.PROP_027_addition: Script must handle: (a) reverting a route-to-analyst closure restores ISS status to whatever prior_status was (typically 'open'); (b) reverting a route-to-curmudgeon closure ALSO removes the queue item with route_queue_id from priority-queue.json (or marks it superseded); (c) reverting a narrow-patch reverts the patch via git (out of scope for the revert script — patch revert is git's job).
revert_script_compatibility.implementation_note: Add helper function `revert_route_to_curmudgeon(ledger_entry)` that calls `node -e` to splice queue.queue array. Idempotent.

Q1_matrix_scope_phased_or_combined.recommendation: BOTH route-to-analyst AND route-to-curmudgeon in a single coherent commit.
rationale_for_combined: (a) Class-propagation discipline must span both paths from day one. Splitting them creates a deployment window where ROUTE-TO-ANALYST works but ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON falls back to ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN, which is the same over-escalation pattern PROP-027 exists to fix.
(b) The matrix's decision tree only makes sense as a whole. Five actions vs three is a single conceptual change; landing it in two phases doubles the prompt-churn cost.

(c) Route-to-curmudgeon is rarely-used (today's 9-ISS batch had zero), so the implementation cost is low. The matrix definition is the bulk of the work; adding the action vs withholding it is a few extra lines.
(d) Operator framing in the directive ('Operator framing says both, but tinker may recommend phased') invited the phased option — tinker considered it and recommends combined for the reasons above.
Q1_matrix_scope_phased_or_combined.if_operator_overrides_to_phased: Phase 1 = ROUTE-TO-ANALYST + class-hint propagation only. Phase 2 (later) = ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON. The matrix decision tree would have step 5 stubbed as 'fallthrough to step 6' until phase 2 lands. Acceptable but strictly worse than combined.

Q2_moderate_narrow_patch_test_rules.recommendation: Three-rule conjunctive gate: NARROWNESS + RE-GREP + NOT_NEVER_PUSH (defined in narrowness_gate section).
Q2_moderate_narrow_patch_test_rules.rationale: Mirrors PROP-026 M2's three-rule conjunctive guard pattern (severity floor + negative-signal blocklist + source provenance). Conjunctive is right for safety-critical gates: ANY rule failure routes to safe default (ROUTE-TO-ANALYST), not unsafe default (auto-patch).
Q2_moderate_narrow_patch_test_rules.operator_review_point: Tinker is most uncertain about the NARROWNESS rule definition — 'single-field, single-line, 2-3-character correction' is the conservative line. If operator wants to widen (e.g., allow 'single-paragraph rewrite' to qualify), the NARROW-PATCH yield rises but so does false-positive risk on moderate severity. Recommend starting conservative; widen later if tinker observes the gate over-rejecting clearly-mechanical moderates.

Q3_default_action_flip.recommendation: YES — flip moderate default from ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN to ROUTE-TO-ANALYST.
evidence_for_flip: Operator manual re-route classified 8/9 as analyst-class work (not operator-only).
The 1/9 that correctly stayed pending-human (ISS-1089) hit NEVER_PUSH and would route correctly under the new matrix anyway via step 4.
Pattern is unlikely to flip on next batch given the historical mix of ISS types in the corpus (most moderates ARE analyst-class).
evidence_against_flip_considered: Single data point. Counter: the underlying cause (no structured route-to-analyst action in PROP-026 matrix) is observable from the matrix definition itself, not just the empirical fire. Even N=1 with this signal strength is sufficient.

Routing complexity may cause LLM errors. Counter: the LLM is already making per-issue judgment; the question is its option set. A 5-action matrix is structurally cleaner than 3-action with 'override to analyst' as an unstructured side channel.
Q3_default_action_flip.verdict: Flip is correct. ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN is now reserved for the genuinely-operator-only cases (NEVER_PUSH, physical-world, legal/strategic).

Q4_class_hint_placement.recommendation: On the ISS itself (`iss.class_hint`), mirrored on the closure-ledger entry (`ledger.class_hint` and `closure_evidence.class_hint` — top-level for revert-script use, evidence-mirror for audit).
Q4_class_hint_placement.rationale: Analyst reads ISS at intake; the hint must be co-located. Closure-ledger mirror preserves the routing decision's hint as historical record, useful for revert and audit. Free-text routing_reason rejected (brittle parsing); ledger-only rejected (analyst doesn't read ledger at intake).

Q5_reference_doc_scope.recommendation: Single new file `monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md` capturing: (a) the 5-action decision tree; (b) the narrowness gate; (c) the class-hint propagation chain; (d) worked examples from today's 9-ISS batch.
consumers: decider-curmudgeon.md Step 8c carry-over enforcement (links for class-hint logic)
analyst-mode1-expansions.md intake addendum (links for class_hint reading)
Q5_reference_doc_scope.NOT_in_scope_for_this_doc: PROP-025 review_class authoring guidance (stays in analyst-mode1-expansions.md per existing PROP-025 architecture). The new doc cross-references PROP-025 but doesn't duplicate the author guidance.

Q6_closure_ledger_schema_extension.recommendation: YES — add 'route-to-analyst', 'route-to-curmudgeon', 'narrow-patch' to action_taken enum; promote action_taken from closure_evidence to top-level. Add per-action fields per the closure_ledger_schema_extension section.
Q6_closure_ledger_schema_extension.rationale: Audit-trail consistency. The revert script needs to distinguish closure types to do the right thing per type (re-open ISS for route-to-analyst; also remove queue item for route-to-curmudgeon; etc.).

Q7_curmudgeon_side_tagging.recommendation: PROP-025's existing analyst-declares-review_class discipline is sufficient. No new helper signals required.
Q7_curmudgeon_side_tagging.rationale: The class_hint added by PROP-027 is upstream-advisory. Once analyst authors the EXP with review_class, the chain runs on PROP-025 rails: decider reads exp.review_class → push queue with class=exp.review_class → curmudgeon reads item.class. No new signal between analyst and curmudgeon. The hint's only effect is reducing analyst's per-issue cognitive load when the routing context already implies a class — and even then, analyst overrides if scope-of-work disagrees with the hint.

implementation_path.single_phase_combined_recommendation: All changes land in one coherent commit; PROP is a unit. Operator approves PROP-027 → tinker (or operator) authors the prompt edits + new doc → commit.

files_to_modify.change: Replace the severity-tier matrix logic with the 5-action decision tree. Bash helper: extend ledger entry to include action_taken at top level + class_hint + route_queue_id when applicable. Add LLM override paths for ROUTE-TO-ANALYST and ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON. Update Daily report `m1_sweep` field shape to include `routed_to_analyst`, `routed_to_curmudgeon`, `narrow_patched` counts.
files_to_modify.size_estimate: ~80 lines added (decision tree + bash helper extensions + LLM override docs); ~30 lines removed (current 3-action default-escalate code path)

files_to_modify.change: Step 8c terminal-action set extends to include ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON (was implicit; PROP-027 makes structured). Add class derivation rule: 'if carry-over is post-patch verification, push class=verification; if substantive re-attack, push class=deep-attack'. Update self-test: each unresolved_prior_cycle entry must have a ledger line with one of {patch, wontfix, escalate, route-to-curmudgeon} from the same RUN_ID.

files_to_modify.section: Intake addendum (after assigned-analyst intake; before EXP authoring)
files_to_modify.change: Add: 'When picking up an `assigned-analyst` ISS, read iss.class_hint along with iss.routing_reason. The hint is advisory — factor into your review_class decision when authoring the EXP, but override per scope-of-work if the hint disagrees with what the EXP actually does.' Reference monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md.

files_to_modify.section: open-issues.json schema (add class_hint, routed_at, routed_by_run, routed_to_curmudgeon_queue_id fields) + closure-ledger.jsonl schema (add action_taken top-level enum + per-action fields)
files_to_modify.change: Document schema extensions per closure_ledger_schema_extension section.
No additions — class_hint and routing fields live on existing files (open-issues.json, closure-ledger.jsonl) which are already correctly classified. Verified.

files_to_create.initial_content_outline:
# M1 Routing Matrix and Class-Hint Propagation

## The 5-action decision tree
[Decision tree from this PROP, formatted as prose + table]

## The narrowness gate (NARROW-PATCH)
[Three rules]

## Class-hint propagation chain
[Step-by-step from this PROP]

## Worked examples from 2026-05-10 9-ISS batch
[Per-ISS walks, abbreviated from this PROP]

## When to route analyst vs curmudgeon
[Source-based defaults table]

## See also: PROP-026 (M1 sweep), PROP-025 (curmudgeon batch gate, review_class authority)

files_NOT_modified_explicitly: monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md — PROP-025 already covers Step 8a class reading; no PROP-027 changes needed
monitor/prompts/decider.md push sites for queue (Step 7 EXP integration) — PROP-025 already covers; M1 ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON path uses the same push pattern
build.js OWNERSHIP table — no new files classified (open-issues.json + closure-ledger.jsonl already there); routing-matrix.md is in monitor/prompts/, already in NEVER_PUSH via wildcard

build-scripts/revert-burndown-closures.js — extension recommended but defer to PROP-027 follow-up commit if revert script extension is needed at deploy time. The base revert (re-open ISS) works for route-to-analyst by default; route-to-curmudgeon revert needs additional logic but that path is rare at first deploy.
deployment_steps: 2. Tinker (or operator) authors the prompt edits + new doc in clean clone.
4. Operator commits PROP-027 changes in a single coherent commit.
5. Mark DIRECTIVE-20260510-002 status=completed via DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE rule (tinker handles in same run as PROP authorship).

6. Watch next M1 fire (next decider run after commit) — verify revised matrix produces a non-100%-escalate distribution. Specifically: of the next 10 candidates, expect ~50-70% ROUTE-TO-ANALYST, ~10-20% ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN, ~10-20% NARROW-PATCH or PATCH or WONTFIX, ~0-5% ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON.

tests_required.test_name: M1 matrix decision tree on the 9 ISS test batch
tests_required.assertion: Given the 9 ISSs from 2026-05-10's first burndown fire as input, the matrix produces: 8 ROUTE-TO-ANALYST, 1 NARROW-PATCH, 1 ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN (counting ISS-1043 as NARROW-PATCH and ISS-1089 separately as ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN; the 9 directive-listed ISSs route 8/9 to analyst with the operator's manual re-route).
tests_required.rationale: The 9-ISS batch is the canonical test set for the matrix. If it doesn't classify them correctly, the matrix definition is broken.

tests_required.assertion: Given (a) a moderate ISS with NARROWNESS=true RE-GREP=true NOT_NEVER_PUSH=true → action=NARROW-PATCH; (b) any one rule failed → action≠NARROW-PATCH; (c) NEVER_PUSH=true on otherwise-narrow → ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN if other criteria match step 4; (d) all-pass on minor → action=PATCH (not NARROW-PATCH).
tests_required.rationale: Gate behavior must be deterministic and conjunctive. Catches gate inversions.

tests_required.assertion: Given an ISS with class_hint='verification', after ROUTE-TO-ANALYST and analyst EXP authoring with review_class='verification', decider's queue push carries class='verification'. Mirror chain for class_hint='deep-attack' and override case (hint='verification' but analyst sets review_class='deep-attack' → queue push class='deep-attack', i.e., review_class is authoritative).
tests_required.rationale: Verifies the chain is structured correctly and PROP-025 invariant holds.

tests_required.assertion: Each line in closure-ledger.jsonl has top-level action_taken ∈ {'patch','narrow-patch','wontfix','route-to-analyst','route-to-curmudgeon','escalate'}. Schema validation fails on any other value.
tests_required.rationale: Forward compatibility with revert script + monthly audit. Catches typos.

gain: Eliminates the 100% over-escalation rate on the first burndown fire (9/9 → ~10% under revised matrix). Operator no longer has to manually re-route analyst-class items.
Adds NARROW-PATCH path for clearly-mechanical moderates — saves 1 analyst hop per occurrence (today's batch: 1 ISS).
Class-hint propagation gives analyst-driven EXPs the right review_class default → throughput gain from PROP-025 batchability extends to the M1 → analyst → curmudgeon chain.
Closure-ledger action_taken enum extension provides clean audit trail for the new actions; revert script can distinguish closure types.
Single new reference doc (routing-matrix.md) consolidates the matrix logic in one place, eliminating duplication between decider.md, decider-curmudgeon.md, and analyst-mode1-expansions.md.
loss: Matrix complexity goes from 3 actions to 5 actions. Decision tree has 6 steps instead of 3. LLM judgment surface area increases.
Closure-ledger schema extension is a forward-only change; old ledger entries don't have action_taken at top level (mitigated: revert script reads closure_evidence.action_intent as fallback).
Class-hint is advisory only — adds a field that's load-bearing only when present; analyst is free to ignore or override. Field nominally adds noise without always paying off.
ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON path may stay rarely used. Carrying the action through prompts and schemas adds maintenance surface area for a rare case.

risks.risk: Single data point (one burndown fire) — premature matrix change
risks.mitigation: The underlying cause (no structured route-to-analyst path) is observable from the matrix definition itself, not just the empirical fire. Even one fire with this signal strength is sufficient. Counter-counter: tinker monthly audit at 30 days verifies the revised matrix's actual distribution; if patterns deviate, propose adjustment.
risks.residual: Low. The fix addresses a definitional gap, not an empirical contingency.

risks.risk: Narrowness gate false-positive — auto-patches a moderate that should have been routed
risks.mitigation: Three-rule conjunctive gate (all must hold). Closure-ledger logs gate result on every closure for audit. Worst case: revert script re-opens the ISS; patch revert via git.
risks.residual: Low-Moderate. The risk is bounded by gate conservatism (single-field/single-line definition); widening the definition would raise this risk.

risks.risk: Class_hint ignored by analyst, becomes noise
risks.mitigation: PROP-025 keeps review_class authoritative on the EXP. The hint adds advisory information; if analyst always overrides, the hint is harmless noise. Tinker monthly audit checks class_hint vs review_class agreement rate; if persistently <50%, deprecate the hint (low cost).
risks.residual: Low. Worst case is reverting to a no-hint world; no degradation vs status quo.

risks.risk: ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON path adds maintenance surface area for a rare case
risks.mitigation: The action is a thin wrapper over an existing queue push pattern. Maintenance cost is the matrix definition entry + decision-tree step 5 + closure-ledger schema extension. ~30 lines total.

risks.risk: PROP-026 was just deployed today — adding PROP-027 immediately may be premature; let PROP-026 settle first
risks.mitigation: PROP-027 amends PROP-026 specifically to fix a gap that fired on day 1. Waiting risks more over-escalations and operator manual re-routes (which themselves have a cost). The fix is targeted; not landing it means continued operator burden.

risks.risk: Step 5 (route-to-curmudgeon) judgment criterion is fuzzy — 'curmudgeon re-attack vs analyst defense' is a subjective call
risks.mitigation: Source-based defaults table provides anchors. LLM judgment per-issue with the table as guidance. Worked example for ISS-1164 (curmudgeon-raised, analyst-defense) shows how the call is made.
risks.residual: Moderate. Tinker monthly audit verifies the call is being made consistently — measure rate of ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON closures and audit a sample for correctness.

honest_uncertainties: Step 5 'next action is curmudgeon re-attack' criterion is subjective; LLM judgment may vary run-to-run. The audit signal (curmudgeon comes back with new findings on the routed target → re-attack was correct; comes back saying 'still open' carryover-style → should have been route-to-analyst) takes weeks to accumulate.

Narrowness gate's NARROWNESS rule — exact line-count threshold (single-line vs 2-3-line vs single-paragraph) is a judgment call. PROP-027 starts conservative (single-field/single-line); operator may want to widen later.
ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON path may go unused. If after 30 days it has zero closures, consider whether the action should be deprecated (code path exists but never fires).

Class_hint at step 6 (default ROUTE-TO-ANALYST) is null-by-default; LLM is asked to populate it 'when uncertain, leave null.' Distribution of populated-vs-null hints is an empirical question post-deploy.

The 'single coherent commit' recommendation assumes tests/run.sh has the new test cases passing pre-commit. If tests can't be added in the same commit (test infrastructure constraints), then either land tests separately first OR use a phased commit (matrix changes commit + test commit in lockstep).

Worked example for ISS-1087 / ISS-1088 (TS-tab framing) hinges on the docs/index.html-NEVER_PUSH sub-rule clarification. This sub-rule needs operator confirmation: are docs/index.html prose patches eligible for M1 narrow-patch via stranded-patches workflow, or are they always ROUTE-TO-ANALYST? PROP-027 currently says 'phrasing judgment fails NARROWNESS rule, so ROUTE-TO-ANALYST regardless' — this is the conservative call but may under-utilize the narrow-patch path.
verification_pattern: grep -q 'ROUTE-TO-ANALYST' monitor/prompts/decider.md && grep -q 'narrowness_gate\|NARROWNESS rule' monitor/prompts/decider.md && grep -q 'class_hint' monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-mode1-expansions.md && test -f monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md && grep -q 'route-to-analyst' monitor/prompts/reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
verification_pattern_explanation: Returns FIXED when all five components landed: (1) decider.md mentions ROUTE-TO-ANALYST as a matrix action; (2) decider.md describes the narrowness gate (NARROWNESS rule); (3) analyst-mode1-expansions.md reads class_hint at intake; (4) the new routing-matrix.md reference doc exists; (5) DATA-SCHEMAS.md documents the route-to-analyst action_taken value. Conjunction ensures no partial deployment.
expected_when_fixed: All five greps + file existence true on origin/main; next M1 fire produces a non-100%-escalate distribution.
follow_up_priors_set_by_this_proposal: Tinker monthly audit (30 days post-deploy) verifies: (a) M1 action distribution — expect <20% ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN, ~50-70% ROUTE-TO-ANALYST, balance NARROW-PATCH/PATCH/WONTFIX/ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON; (b) narrowness gate pass-rate on moderates — expect ~10-30% pass; lower means too conservative, much higher means risk-of-false-positive; (c) class_hint vs review_class agreement rate — if <50%, consider deprecating the hint; (d) ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON closures count — if zero, reconsider whether the action is needed.
Operator-facing weekly summary (already in latest-decider-summary.txt) should include `m1_sweep` action distribution so operator can spot drift early.

test_case_ISSs_with_routing_reason: monitor/decisions/open-issues.json — search for routing_reason field on ISS-1043, ISS-1087, ISS-1088, ISS-1130, ISS-1132, ISS-1147, ISS-1164, ISS-1165, ISS-1166

why_human_needed: Operator must approve: (a) the 5-action decision tree as the revised matrix; (b) the narrowness gate's three rules (especially the NARROWNESS rule's conservatism — single-field/single-line); (c) the default-action flip from ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN to ROUTE-TO-ANALYST; (d) class_hint placement on the ISS field (vs alternatives); (e) the bundled-vs-phased deployment choice; (f) routing-matrix.md as a new reference file vs inline duplication.
implementation_files: monitor/prompts/reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md (M1 routing fields + ledger schema extensions)
